text: What softened you this week?
Describe a small kindness you noticed this week — one you didn't say thank you for.
author: Mary Oliver

text: What have you been carrying lately that you haven't named out loud?
Who are you becoming?
What would you tell yourself six months ago?
Write about something you've been avoiding.
What did silence sound like today?
Describe the quality of light this morning.
What feels unfinished?
What did you learn about yourself this week?
Write about a time you changed your mind.
What are you grateful for that you rarely say aloud?
Describe a moment of unexpected beauty from the past few days.
What's a conversation you keep having with yourself?
What does home feel like to you right now?
Write about something that surprised you this week.
What are you protecting?
Describe a smell that takes you somewhere.
What would you do if you weren't afraid?
Write about a person who has shaped how you see the world.
What do you wish someone understood about you?
Describe the last time you felt completely at ease.
What are you holding onto that you might need to let go?
Write about a decision you made that still resonates.
What small ritual makes your day feel more like yours?
What do you want to remember about right now?
Write about a fear that has shrunk with time.
What does rest actually feel like to you?
Describe a moment when words weren't enough.
What question are you sitting with lately?
Write about a place you return to in your mind.
What have you forgiven yourself for recently?
What is asking for your attention right now?
Describe the texture of this season.
What would you say to the version of yourself from five years ago?
Write about something ordinary that moved you.
What are you learning to receive?
Describe a moment of laughter that you want to keep.
What does it feel like to be in your own company?
Write about something that has stayed with you longer than expected.
What do you want more of in your days?
Describe the last meal you really tasted.
What truth are you just beginning to accept?
Write about a kindness you received that you still think about.
What has this year taught you about who you are?
Describe a sound that makes you feel safe.
What boundary did you hold that cost you something?
Write about a time you felt understood.
What is the most honest thing you could say about yourself right now?
Describe the moment you realized something had changed.
What do you want to stop explaining to people?
Write about something you love that few people know about.
What would a more patient version of you do differently?
Describe the last time you were fully present.
What are you still grieving?
Write about the way a particular person enters a room.
What has your body been trying to tell you?
Describe a moment that was better than you expected.
What would enough feel like?
Write about a word that has changed meaning for you.
What does belonging feel like to you?
Describe a morning you want to remember.
What are you practicing being?
Write about something in nature that has stayed with you.
What are you finally letting yourself want?
Describe the weight of something you've been carrying.
What has changed about how you love people?
Write about a time you chose yourself.
What is one thing you are proud of that you rarely say aloud?
Describe the last walk you took — where did your mind go?
What does courage look like in your ordinary days?
Write about someone you miss in a way that surprises you.
What are you still learning how to say?
Describe a moment when everything felt exactly right.
What have you stopped pretending?
Write about a hope you are still holding.
What would a future version of you want you to begin today?
Describe something that has always been true about you.
What are you grateful for today that you wouldn't have noticed a year ago?
Write about a time something ended better than it started.
What is patience teaching you right now?
Describe a relationship that has grown in unexpected ways.
What are you no longer afraid of?
Write about a dream — waking or sleeping — that has stayed with you.
What do you notice about the way you treat yourself?
Describe the beginning of something.
What are you learning to value that you once overlooked?
Write about a conversation that shifted something in you.
What does it feel like when you are most yourself?
Describe something you have made with your hands.
What are you willing to be wrong about?
Write about the quiet between two things.
What does this season ask of you?
Describe a small moment of grace from this week.
What are you beginning to understand about how you grieve?
Write about a smell, sound, or texture that carries a year with it.
What has love changed about the way you see yourself?
Describe the feeling of returning to something familiar.
What are you in the middle of right now?
